response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned polyglot engineer with genuine strengths in testing, distributed web services, and developer tooling, but he falls critically short of the core minimum qualifications for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++ experience; his resume shows roughly 6 months of C++ at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project — C++ is not even listed in his skills section. Similarly, the required 5 years of large-scale infrastructure or cloud compute experience is not demonstrated; his closest work is ~2 years on high-throughput HTTP micro-services and some AWS CDK usage. There is no evidence of formal technical leadership over cloud or infrastructure teams, and the overall trajectory is firmly web/application-layer rather than compute platform engineering.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture: deep TDD practice, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source testing tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) across multiple roles.
Breadth of distributed systems exposure at web scale: Dunnhumby micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and high-performance databases.
Consistent staff/senior-level ownership and community contribution: conference talks, full-day workshops, and founding an open-source Geo-Data platform used in production by NGOs.
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments + a thesis project) against the 8-year hard minimum; this alone is disqualifying.
No demonstrated experience leading or mentoring a cloud infrastructure or platform engineering team; leadership evidence is limited to community workshops and open-source projects.
Cloud compute platform depth is absent: no kernel, hypervisor, storage, or hardware architecture work; AWS experience is limited to CDK for a pricing service, not platform-layer infrastructure.
